exemplar.title:
To what extent can integration and differential equations develop an accurate
tire degradation model for modern F1 cars, with factors including track temperature, tire compound, and track geometry and elevation?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent can integration and differential equations develop an accurate
tire degradation model for modern F1 cars, with factors including track temperature, tire compound, and track geometry and elevation?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear three-level structure with numbered headings and a contents page guiding the reader
Overall readable flow that supports the main storyline
weaknesses: Frequent digressions and repetition interrupt coherence
Mis-numbered and inconsistent headings, empty sub-headings
Verbose sections and typographical errors detract from conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the exploration is well organized and structured (band 3), uneven logical development and unnecessary material pull it back from the highest level. On balance, it fits the lower half of band 3, earning 3 marks.

strengths: Use of standard calculus symbols (â«, dT/dt, ln) and display-mode equations
A variables table clarifies symbol meanings
weaknesses: Notation mis-uses (e.g. conflicting definitions of T and C, misplaced absolute-value bars)
Limited student-generated representations; heavy reliance on clipped images
Logical gaps in the argument (treating variables as constants without explanation)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows some consistent mathematical language and logical layout but is held back by inconsistent notation, sparse original graphs and unexplained steps. This aligns with band 2, awarding 2 marks.

strengths: Personal passion for Formula 1 clearly informs the modelling framework
Independent derivation of four linked differential equations
Evidence of research and simulator testing to choose constants
weaknesses: Model structure largely follows a familiar template
Limited novelty in the mathematical approach
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach (band 2), but the exploration lacks the originality or innovation required for the top band, so it scores 2 marks.

strengths: Meaningful evaluation of outcomes against simulator telemetry
Clear identification of limitations and suggestions for improvement
Thoughtful discussion of why different tracks yield different wear rates
weaknesses: Reflection lacks quantitative error analysis (e.g. residuals or percentage errors)
Limited depth in critiquing parameter-estimation methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and shows thoughtful analysis and evaluation (band 2), but it stops short of the deep, searching critique needed for band 3, hence 2 marks.

strengths: Appropriate use of differential equations and integration techniques
Mathematics consistently at or above SL course level
Clear demonstration of methods such as separation of variables
weaknesses: Standard textbook derivations unrelated to the model consume space
Conceptual errors (e.g. variable mis-labelling, missing negative signs)
Incomplete parameter values and symbol misuse in tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration applies relevant SL-level mathematics effectively and demonstrates a good degree of technical competence (approaching band 4). Although errors and extraneous material prevent the top two bands, the overall mathematical use warrants 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths** â Derivations are laid out step-by-step and paragraphs try to justify each manipulation, so a reader can usually follow the algebra.

**Weaknesses** â Logical gaps remain (e.g., treating T as a constant while integrating, unexplained selection of numerical constants).  Occasional mis-numbering and symbol clashes blur the argument.

Consequently the strand shows âsome logical structure and clarityâ â band 2.

While individual algebraic steps are shown, several conceptual errors exist (treating T as both variable and constant, missing negative signs, incorrect exponential rearrangements).  Numeric substitutions are copied without checking units.  Understanding is partial and there are non-trivial errors.

Therefore the work fits band 2 (âsome understanding with errorsâ).

**Evidence of representations** â The student provides formulae, two small graphs (area under curve and Riemann rectangles), tables of variables, tyre-compound icons and circuit maps.

**Limitations** â Only one graph is produced by the studentâs own work; the others are clipped images.  No dynamic or technology-generated plots of the final model are shown, so the representations do not substantially enhance understanding.

Thus the work achieves âsome use of different formsâ, placing it in band 2.

**Strengths** â The exploration follows a recognisable overall flow (background â methodology â modelling â testing â conclusion).  Headings and a contents page guide the reader and the modelling steps are, in broad terms, presented in a logical sequence.

**Weaknesses** â Frequent digressions (e.g. two-page lesson on basic integration, lengthy general history of F1) interrupt the argument and give the piece a âstop-startâ feel.  Links between sections are often weak or implicit, so the reader must infer why an example is present.  Repetition (multiple explanations of the same separation-of-variables process) and typographical errors further disrupt coherence.

Because the work is readable and the main storyline can be followed, but logical development is uneven and sometimes forced, band 2 (âsome coherenceâ) is the best fit.

Creating four linked differential models and applying them to two tracks, plus sourcing real-world constants, shows a respectable level of creativity and initiative.  The modelling template remains fairly simple and some constants are guessed, so the creativity is good rather than exceptional.

Band 2 (âgood level of creativity and initiativeâ) fits best.

There is thoughtful, if brief, analysis of how well the predicted degradation matches simulator results and why Singapore wears tyres faster than Spa.  The student also questions constant-factor assumptions.  Analysis is sensible but not deeply mathematical or probing.

Hence the work fits band 2 (âthoughtful analysis of results and processâ).

Outcomes are evaluated against simulator telemetry and the student proposes specific improvements (add marble build-up, include driver style).  Future implications are noted, though details are light.

This satisfies the descriptor for band 2 (âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ).

The student reflects on the realism of outputs (e.g., compares calculated 2.48 % wear with simulator data, discusses pit-stop timing) and acknowledges learning gains.  Reflection appears in the testing sections and in a separate âLimitationsâ table, indicating meaningful reflection throughout.

Critical depth is moderate rather than searching, so band 2 is the best descriptor.

**Strengths** â Standard calculus symbols (â«, dT/dt, ln | |) are employed, variables are listed in a table, and equations are typeset in display mode.

**Weaknesses** â Notation is frequently mis-used (Î¼ defined as âfrictional force Nâ; T appears simultaneously as track temperature and tyre life; constants called C in several unrelated contexts).  Absolute value bars are incorrectly placed in the final formulas.  Such inconsistencies show only âsomeâ appropriate use.

Band 2 is therefore appropriate.

The choice of a personal passion (Formula 1) and the decision to validate the model inside a racing simulator demonstrate a clear personal approach.  Narrative comments such as âas an IB student who has been watching this sportâ reinforce ownership.  Nonetheless, the approach is not especially novel in mathematical terms.

Therefore band 2 is appropriate.

**Strengths** â Most paragraphs relate to the research question; the student eventually applies every piece of mathematics to the modelling task.

**Weaknesses** â Large sections (basic differentiation vs integration, Riemann-sum derivation) add little to the modelling goal and inflate the page count.  Explanations are often verbose and repeat earlier material, so the exploration is not particularly economical.

The work is therefore âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ, matching band 2.

Relevant mathematics (differential equations, integration, logarithms) is indeed present, but large portions are standard textbook demonstrations unrelated to the model, and the core tyre-wear formula is introduced without derivation from physics.  Application is therefore limited.

Band 2 â âsome relevant mathematics with limited applicationâ â is appropriate.

**Evidence** â The student devises a personalised modelling framework (four differential equations) rather than copying an existing model, showing noticeable independent thought.  Constants are selected after research into F1 data and personal simulator testing.

**However** the derivations largely mirror a single template and rely on a basic logistic-style form, so independence is significant but not outstanding.

This aligns with band 2 (âsignificant evidence of independent thinkingâ).

**Strengths** â A clear three-level structure (sections, subsections, numbered headings) plus a table of contents shows deliberate organisation.  Key parts (Introduction, Methodology, Testing, Limitations) occur in an expected order and page references are mostly correct.

**Weaknesses** â Several headings are mis-numbered, some items listed in the contents do not appear exactly as titled, and tables/figures are inserted without consistent captions.  Nonetheless, the basic introduction-body-conclusion framework is evident and sustained.

Given the visible planning but noticeable execution errors, the strand aligns with band 3 (âwell organised with clear structureâ).

Techniques used (separation of variables, integration of rational functions, exponential solutions) are standard HL level and are generally appropriate for the task.  No material goes beyond HL, but the mathematics is solidly at course level throughout.

Thus band 3 (âmathematics commensurate with course levelâ) is justified.

annotations.comment: The âFun Factâ box interrupts the technical discussion. Consider its relevance or move it to an appendix.
The title is overly long and combines multiple elements, which reduces immediacy and clarity. Consider streamlining to focus on the core topic and research question.
The research question is clearly stated and directly aligned with the explorationâs aims, providing strong focus for the subsequent methodology.
Entries in the Table of Contents are mis-numbered and some headings do not match the main text. Ensure consistency between contents and section titles.
The historical overview of Formula 1 in the introduction is lengthy and only loosely connected to the mathematical focus. Trim this to improve coherence.
Personal reflections on your passion for F1 lend authenticity and demonstrate clear ownership of the exploration topic.
There is an empty sub-heading here, which interrupts flow. Remove or replace it with appropriate content to maintain structure.
The list of factors affecting tire degradation lacks clear linkage to the subsequent models. Explicitly state how each factor enters your equations.
These images are informative but lack captions, making it hard for the reader to know what they represent. Add descriptive captions and figure numbers.
The result â0.0833 unitÂ²â is presented without discussing units or approximation error. Explain the unit derivation and rounding.
The Riemann-sum summation indices and Îx are not defined; clarify the general form $$\sum_{i=1}^n \Delta x\,f(x_i)$$ with explicit limits.
The citation displays HTML artifacts and inconsistent formatting. Clean up footnotes and ensure uniform referencing.
The extensive textbook comparison between differentiation and integration could be condensed; focus on the novel aspects relevant to your model.
The placeholder âExample:â table is empty and interrupts the narrative. Remove or populate it with meaningful content.
Relying on technology for simple arithmetic undercuts the mathematical argument. Show the Riemann sum calculation manually for clarity.
The methodology list is concise and provides a clear roadmap for model construction and testing.
In the differential equation $$\frac{dT}{dt}=\mu T t\left(1-\frac{T}{100}\right)$$, T is used for both dependent variable and track temperature. Rename one for clarity.
The final expression includes the term $e^{(\mu T/2)t^2+C\over100}$ without discussing how constants are chosen. Reflect on parameter estimation methods.
Several integrals are enclosed in absolute-value notation. Since $T<100$ during a lap, absolute bars may be unnecessaryâconsider removing them.
The example differential equation $$\frac{dy}{dx}=3x5y$$ omits a multiplication operator. Correct it to $$\frac{dy}{dx}=15xy$$ for precision.
Variables table uses generic letters C, G, E which may be confused with constants. Assign meaningful symbols (e.g., Î¸ for camber) and define them clearly.
The separation-of-variables step is laid out correctly, helping the reader follow the integration process.
The section header â5.4â is misaligned with the previous numbering. Ensure all headings are numbered consistently.
In the camber differential equation, the symbol $C$ is used both for camber angle and integration constant. Rename one symbol to avoid confusion.
The âCoefficient of frictionâ entry is blank in the circuit data list. Provide the numeric value to complete the model inputs.
The narrative on comparing simulator outputs is descriptive but lacks quantitative error analysis. Include percentage errors or residuals to strengthen validation.
In the Marina Bay circuit data list, the frictional force entry lacks value. Ensure all relevant parameters are provided for reproducibility.
The limitations table effectively summarizes errors and improvements, showing clear critical reflection on model constraints.
The âUniformity of variablesâ row lists no possible improvement. Propose a specific strategy (e.g., time-varying functions) to address this limitation.
The conclusion recapitulates the work but is overly verbose. Condense into key findings and explicitly link back to the research question.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (italics, URLs, authors). Adopt a uniform IB referencing style throughout.
